name: From Constantina to San Nicolás del Puerto - circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park
name_original: Desde Constantina a San Nicolás del Puerto – circular por el Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Constantina offer diverse terrain within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features a mix of pastures, cork oak forests, and mountainous landscapes, providing varied cycling experiences. Cyclists can navigate greenways built on former railway lines and low-traffic roads with numerous curves. The area includes both ascending and descending sections, catering to different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Constantina The most popular road cycling route is From Constantina to San Nicolás del Puerto - circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the varied ecosystems of the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Lora del Río – Castle of Constantina loop from Constantina, a difficult 62.3 miles (100.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Sevillian countryside and passes by the Castle of Constantina. Local road cyclists also love the Isla Margarita Recreation Area – Cazalla de la Sierra loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a 44.8 miles (72.0 km) trail leading through the Isla Margarita Recreation Area and near Cazalla de la Sierra, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Constantina is defined by pastures, cork oak forests, and greenways. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Constantina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Constantina?
A: The Constantina region, situated within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter a mix of rolling pastures, dense cork oak forests, and mountainous sections. Many routes utilize greenways built on former railway lines, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Norte de Sevilla Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, and low-traffic roads with numerous curves. The terrain includes both ascending and descending tendencies, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Constantina area?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Constantina documented on komoot, catering to various skill levels and preferences. These routes allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: What are the difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Constantina?
A: The routes around Constantina offer a range of difficulty levels. You can find options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides. Specifically, there are 2 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Constantina?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Constantina are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. For example, the challenging route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994018505/from-constantina-to-san-nicolas-del-puerto-circular-through-the-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Constantina to San Nicolás del Puerto - circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, traversing varied ecosystems.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Constantina's routes are rich with natural beauty and points of interest. You can cycle past unique rock formations at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, explore the well-preserved riverside forests of Ribera de Ciudadeja, or experience the stunning autumn colors along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4766000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castañares Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/736496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar River Landscape&lt;/a&gt; in Cazalla de la Sierra is also a notable feature.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints accessible by road bike in Constantina?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can enjoy breathtaking views from several points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas of the unique rock formations. Another stunning spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Buendía Reservoir from Mirador de Las Palomas&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive water and landscape views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Constantina?
A: The Constantina region is generally pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable. Autumn is particularly spectacular for routes like the Castañares Trail, when the chestnut leaves change color.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through charming towns or offer cultural experiences?
A: Yes, several routes connect to or pass through charming local towns. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lora del Río – Castle of Constantina loop from Constantina&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Sevillian countryside and passes by the historic Castle of Constantina. Other routes might lead to towns like Las Navas de la Concepción, allowing you to experience local culture.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Constantina?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from rolling pastures to cork oak forests, and the engaging routes that include both challenging climbs and rewarding descents within the beautiful Sierra Norte Natural Park.

Q: Can I find routes that include lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region around Constantina features several beautiful lakes and reservoirs that can be incorporated into your cycling adventures. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Azul&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and tranquil spots along your ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance or advanced road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Constantina offers several challenging routes for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Calixto Fountain – San Calixto loop from Constantina&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 139.5 km path with significant elevation changes, perfect for those seeking a demanding ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Castle of Constantina loop from Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 96.4 km route.
tips: The Moorish-era castle was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985. It can be easily reached on foot and by car. The panoramic views of the town are impressive.
The place conditioned as a recreational area, has tables and benches, bridges and wooden walkways. Good place to rest in the shade, thanks to its dense vegetation (elms, poplars and alders).

Beautiful road that allows you to enjoy the dense vegetation of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. Route parallel to the Rivera de Huéznar and the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte. 14 km of the slight climb (up to 3%) between the Cazalla-Constantina train station and the municipality of San Nicolás del Puerto.
